items.briefing:
Business email compromise (BEC) remains the most common cyber incident affecting organisations right now, so staff should stay alert to suspicious emails requesting payments or changes to account details. Separately, a phishing campaign is actively targeting universities, which is relevant to our sector — be cautious with unexpected emails, even from seemingly legitimate sources. Australian organisations handling customer data should also be aware that upcoming AML (anti-money laundering) reforms may require a closer look at how that data is stored and managed.

On the malware front, fake AI apps are being used to spread harmful software — only download applications from official, trusted sources. **Tip:** Before clicking any link or downloading any app, verify it came from an official website rather than a search ad or unsolicited email.

featured.title: Origin Energy confirms customer data breach after unauthorised access
featured.quote: Major Australian energy provider confirms customer data breach, directly affecting millions of Australians.
